threat_model: A reasoning admission binding can accidentally become runtime authority if operator intent, canonical loop identity, or evidence references are conflated with execution. The main risks are authorizing runtime reasoning, registering a loop, transitioning loop status, calling connectors, appending receipts, exporting private reasoning, or claiming terminal closure without live evidence. The mitigation is a schema-backed read-only binding that denies all runtime authorities and keeps runtime promotion blocked on explicit evidence refs.

required_checks.check: runtime reasoning, loop registration, status transition, mutation route, and live adapter authority remain denied
redaction check confirms secret access and private reasoning export remain denied
policy bypass check confirms runtime promotion remains AwaitingEvidence
receipt integrity check confirms runtime blockers and contract summary counts cannot drift
audit visibility check confirms schema, validator, SDLC sidecars, and proof labels are retained

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode binding denies runtime reasoning, loop registration, status transition, connector calls, receipt append, learning updates, terminal closure, and success claims.

mitigations: keep the binding read-only and Foundation Mode scoped
deny runtime reasoning and loop registration authority
deny connector calls, network calls, secret access, and external execution
deny receipt append, learning updates, terminal closure, and success claims
require UAO admission, operator approval, runtime execution design, rollback recovery, live receipts, and terminal closure review before promotion
